data.title: Refactor sprint-next-batch.sh to use ticket next-batch (and ticket list-descendants)
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Refactor `sprint-next-batch.sh` to call `ticket next-batch` instead of reading `.tickets-tracker/` directly. Preserve full flag surface, output format, and exit codes so upstream callers require no update. If the script becomes a thin wrapper, retain its filename for shim compatibility and add it to the boundary hook allowlist with a comment citing this epic.

**Why**: Moves the ~963-line script to a thin CLI wrapper. Eliminates the largest direct `.tickets-tracker/` accessor while preserving the production behavior that sprint orchestration depends on.

**Scope**:
- IN: sprint-next-batch.sh refactor to use `.claude/scripts/dso ticket next-batch` (and `ticket list-descendants` where BFS is needed), full flag/output/exit-code preservation, existing test suite passes with no behavioral regression, boundary-hook allowlist entry with ticket citation
- OUT: Refactoring sprint-next-batch.sh callers themselves (none should need updating — the wrapper preserves the contract); changes to `ticket next-batch` itself (that is epic 2c28-e5b9's scope)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint-next-batch.sh invokes `.claude/scripts/dso ticket next-batch` (via the shim) for candidate selection and hierarchy walk, preserving all output tokens (SKIPPED_*, EPIC:, AVAILABLE_POOL:, BATCH_SIZE:, TASK:), the --json output schema, and the exit-code contract
  ← Satisfies: "sprint-next-batch.sh calls ticket next-batch (preserving full flag surface — flags, output format, and exit codes are identical so callers require no update)"
- When this story is complete, sprint-next-batch.sh's existing test suite (test-sprint-next-batch.sh) passes with no behavioral regression
  ← Satisfies: "Each refactored script passes its existing test suite with no behavioral regression"
- When this story is complete, sprint-next-batch.sh is added to the boundary hook allowlist with an inline comment citing epic e1b2-2653 as the tracking ticket
  ← Satisfies: "Scripts that become thin wrappers retain their filename for shim compatibility and are added to the boundary hook allowlist with a comment citing this epic"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] The external contract (flags, output tokens, exit codes, --json schema) MUST be preserved — upstream sprint/SKILL.md consumes all of these and any regression silently breaks sprint orchestration
- [Dependency] Depends on `ticket next-batch` and `ticket list-descendants` from epic 2c28-e5b9 (inter-epic dependency at the epic level)
- [Testing] Run existing test-sprint-next-batch.sh before and after to diff output on the same fixture — expect byte-for-byte equivalence

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
